The rose plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which can aid in reducing stress, improving sleep, and soothing skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of rose include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Rose is commonly used in herbal preparations such as tea, tincture, and infused oil, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and improve skin health.
